project.name: Dunn Residence
project.current_gate: Licensed CAD runtime activation and written authorization
project.priority: Complete the three floor-plan deliverables before optional automation

milestones.name: Discovery and scope alignment
milestones.detail: Project objective and three-sheet pilot defined.

milestones.name: Source package received
milestones.detail: CAD, PDF, LiDAR and supporting files received and inventoried.

milestones.name: Source intake and duplicate audit
milestones.detail: 908 files / 8.20 GB inventoried; two expected duplicate groups identified.

milestones.name: Scan visual index
milestones.detail: Five scan sessions indexed with client-safe contact sheets.

milestones.name: LAS header audit
milestones.detail: Five LAS files read successfully; format, scale, extents and counts recorded.

milestones.name: LAS core geometry audit
milestones.detail: Robust core extents and outlier inflation analyzed for all sessions.

milestones.name: Registration overlay audit
milestones.detail: Shared-coordinate evidence and pairwise XY overlap documented.

milestones.name: Client report publication
milestones.detail: Five client-safe reports published in the workspace.

milestones.name: AutoCAD and ReCap licensing
milestones.detail: Application licenses are pending on CAD Guardian's execution environment.

milestones.name: BDPC standards dependencies
milestones.detail: Confirm separately maintained CTB/STB, nonstandard fonts and preferred title-block dependency only if applicable.

milestones.name: Commercial authorization
milestones.detail: Approve the $3,200 fixed-fee scope and one consolidated review cycle.

milestones.name: Controlled CAD working set
milestones.detail: Create working copies, validate DWG openability, bind source hierarchy and preserve originals.

milestones.name: Existing floor plan
milestones.detail: Draft and dimension the controlling existing-condition plan first.

milestones.name: Proposed floor plan
milestones.detail: Coordinate rear addition, living/kitchen changes and deck relationship.

milestones.name: Site and area plan
milestones.detail: Document footprint, proposed work, deck/porch context and agreed areas without implying survey services.

milestones.name: Internal drawing QA
milestones.detail: Run geometry, annotation, layer, dimension, viewport and plotting checks.

milestones.name: BDPC check set
milestones.detail: Issue coordinated three-sheet review PDF within three business days of production start.

milestones.name: Consolidated BDPC review
milestones.detail: Receive and resolve one consolidated redline cycle.

milestones.name: Final DWG/PDF issue
milestones.detail: Issue final native DWGs, coordinated PDFs, change summary and unresolved-condition record.

milestones.name: Archive and next-engagement closeout
milestones.detail: Archive reusable assets, confirm acceptance and define any follow-on scope.

file_groups.notes: Client-safe count only; raw imagery is not broadly exposed outside approved report contact sheets.
Raw scanner working files retained privately and unchanged.

file_groups.group: Text, CSV and JSON support files
file_groups.notes: Calibration, trajectory, metadata and derived analysis inputs.
All five files opened successfully during header and geometry audits.
One Dunn production candidate and two BDPC reference drawings; licensed openability check remains.
Dunn conceptual package and BDPC reference PDF.

standards.rule: No finer than 1/2 inch unless BDPC directs otherwise.
Start typical 2×4 framed partitions at 3.5 inches, then reconcile field evidence.
Reuse established BDPC/current-project blocks and known sizes before creating new content.
Use the controlling Dunn CAD and scan evidence; BDPC reference files provide standards context only.
Use the current BDPC presentation standard. Send the preferred title-block DWG only if maintained separately.
No separately maintained CTB/STB was found. Provide only if the controlling set depends on one.
No separately maintained SHX/TTF/OTF package was found. Provide only if required.
Attach scans at native coordinates before any transformation; validate common features in licensed runtime.
Maintain unambiguous condition hierarchy, lineweight and annotation separation.
Do not invent concealed conditions. Flag material conflicts and return architectural decisions to BDPC.
Do not imply survey, civil or field-verification services; label basis and limitations.

automation.result: 908 files cataloged; source groups and privacy flags recorded.
Two expected scanner-package duplicate groups documented.
Five sessions indexed; contact sheets produced with zero image-read failures.

automation.tool: Python standard library / LAS header parsing
automation.result: Five LAS headers read; 91.69M points recorded; zero read errors.
Robust geometry and density images generated for five sessions.
Combined occupancy and pairwise overlap evidence generated.
Run after license activation; does not modify originals.
Use only if it reduces current drawing effort; do not delay floor-plan production.
Validate layers, text, dimensions, viewports and plot output before issue.
Package final DWG/PDF deliverables and dependency record.
Generate at final issue from actual approved changes.
Excluded from the current production path unless separately approved; manual drafting remains controlling.

deliverables.name: Existing floor plan
deliverables.scope: Measured existing-condition plan; conflicts documented rather than concealed.

deliverables.name: Proposed floor plan
deliverables.scope: Rear addition, living/kitchen planning and deck relationship coordinated to BDPC direction.

deliverables.name: Site and area plan
deliverables.scope: Footprint, proposed work, deck/porch context and agreed areas without survey representation.

qa_checks.evidence: Five of five LAS files read successfully; zero read errors.
Meters inferred at 90% heuristic confidence; confirm in licensed viewer.
Strong evidence across building scans; confirm common features before drafting.
Run in licensed AutoCAD before production.
Confirm from controlling DWGs and BDPC dependency response.
Exterior walls, partitions, openings, stairs, fixtures and principal dimensions.
Addition interfaces, room planning, openings and deck connection.
Footprint, work limits, area labels and no-survey disclaimer.
Layers, lineweights, text, dimensions, blocks and condition hierarchy.
Sheet size, title block, viewport scale, page setup and plot behavior.
Three coordinated sheets and review notes.
One BDPC review cycle resolved and logged.
Native DWGs, PDFs, dependency record, change summary and unresolved-condition log.

commercial.status: Due after written authorization and before production start

commercial.value: Within 3 business days of production start
4–5 business days, excluding BDPC review time

runtime.purpose: Required to open/validate DWGs, draft sheets and plot.
Required for LAS indexing/inspection and compatible point-cloud workflow.
Used for intake and audit scripts.
Used for LAS point access and geometry analysis.
Used for robust extents, density and overlap calculations.
Available for PDF inspection and report preparation.
Used for visual indexes and derived images.
Reports, working, QA and delivery folders established.
May accelerate bounded tasks; human CAD review controls issued drawings.
